title: VocoderEffect
description: Phase-vocoder-style effect implemented as a WorkletEffect. Analyzes the spectral envelope of a `modulator` AudioBus across a bank of log-spaced bandpass filters and applies that envelope to the carrier signal (the main input). The result is the classic "robot voice" or talking-synth effect. Band count, frequency range, and Q are compile-time parameters set at construction; only `wet` and `envelopeSmoothing` are adjustable at runtime.

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: Phase-vocoder-style effect implemented as a WorkletEffect. Analyzes the spectral envelope of a `modulator` AudioBus across a bank of log-spaced bandpass filters and applies that envelope to the carrier signal (the main input). The result is the classic "robot voice" or talking-synth effect. Band count, frequency range, and Q are compile-time parameters set at construction; only `wet` and `envelopeSmoothing` are adjustable at runtime.

members.name: _onWorkletReady
members.description: Subclass hook — called once the worklet is loaded and inserted into the wet path. Use for additional wiring (e.g. a sidechain input).

members.name: destroy
members.description: Disconnects all nodes, cancels any pending worklet load, and releases resources.

members.name: envelopeSmoothing
members.description: One-pole envelope follower coefficient. Smaller values produce slower, smoother envelope tracking. Range 0.0001..0.1, default 0.005.

members.name: inputNode
members.description: The node where audio enters this effect.

members.name: outputNode
members.description: The node where audio exits this effect.

members.name: ready
members.description: Resolves once the worklet is loaded and inserted into the wet path.

members.name: wet
members.description: Wet (processed) mix level, 0..1. The dry level is automatically 1 - wet. Ramped smoothly.
